question.content: For the ideal symmetric differential amplifier, estimate emitter quiescent current and dynamic metrics. Output exactly five comma-separated values in the order IEQ_mA,Ad,Ac,Ri_kOhm,Ro_kOhm; keep IEQ_mA to three digits after the decimal point and keep Ad, Ac, Ri_kOhm, Ro_kOhm to one digit after the decimal point; plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no percent sign, no fraction, no equation form, and no extra text.

step_1.content: Build one complete hybrid-pi nodal matrix for the differential pair and load network.
step_1.key_point: No odd-mode/even-mode half-circuit decomposition is used.

step_3.content: Compute IEQ from DC bias, then substitute gm and rpi into the matrix coefficients.
step_3.key_point: All AC metrics are extracted from one linearized parameter set.

step_5.content: Solve the matrix under differential excitation to obtain Ad and Ri.
step_5.key_point: Gain and input resistance come from the same nodal solve.

step_7.content: Change boundary excitation to common-mode and output test conditions to obtain Ac and Ro.
step_7.key_point: Only source constraints change; model equations stay identical.

step_9.content: Apply required rounding and output order IEQ,Ad,Ac,Ri,Ro.
step_9.key_point: Return the exact comma-separated token list.
